calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-07-2023 Off Calendar

Notice Of Default Judgment Prove-Up Hearing As To Defendants Mill Creek Center Holdings, Llc And Joseph W. Battisto (Notices)
Off Calendar May-12-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for notice of default judgment prove-up hearing.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 01:43:48 p.m. and concluding at 02:02:09 p.m.

Plaintiff Orion First Financial, L.L.C., a Washington Limited Liability Company represented by Mark M. Scott, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Mill Creek Center Holdings, LLC a Michigan Limited Liability Company not appearing.
Defendant Joseph W. Battisto not appearing.

MATTHEW JACOBS, Senior Litigation Paralegal/Custodian of Records is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff via Zoom Video Conference.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case and an offer of proof.

The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 1 - Equipment Financing Agreement
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 4 - Personal Guaranty
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 5 - Collection Agency Agreement
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 6 - Payment History
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 7 - Demand Letter
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 8 - Balance of Unpaid Payments + 10% Assessment

MATTHEW JACOBS is examined by the Court. Witness attests that its Declaration filed on Feb-21-2023, statements made by its counsel are true and correct, and the Complaint filed on Aug-03-2022 is the operative pleading.

Court examines Plaintiff's Counsel regarding service of Individual Defendant Joseph W. Battisto. The Court i satisfied with Substituted Service on Defendant.

The matter is submitted.
Court finds the following:

1. Complaint was served on Oct-03-2022 and proof of service filed on Aug-29-2022
2. DOES were dismissed on Feb-21-2023
3. Default entered as to Defendants on Feb-21-2023

Based on the Declaration of Matthew Jacobs filed on Feb-21-2023 with exhibits and the evidence presented, Court finds Plaintiff as proven each essential element of its complaint. Defendants are in breach of the contract agreement, failed to make any payments, and is still in possession of equipment.

After consideration and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Orion First Financial, L.L.C., a Washington Limited Liability Company and against Defendants Mill Creek Center Holdings, LLC a Michigan Limited Liability Company, and Joseph W. Battisto, as follows:

1. Damages: $112,157.71
2. Attorney Fees: $2,751.39
3. Costs: $1,234.75

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $116,143.85

Orion is to recover from Defendants Mill Creek Center Holdings, LLC and Joseph W. Battisto the following personal property: One (1) 2020 GEHL VT320 s/n GHLVT320G00040736. If Orion obtains possession of this personal property and sells it at a sale, Orion shall apply the proceeds from any such sale, net of any such sale and recovery costs and expenses, to reduce the amount owed by Mill Creek Center Holdings, LLC and Joseph W. Battisto under this Judgment after such sale has occurred.

Proposed judgment signed.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-14-2023 Continued To Jul-05-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-15-2022 Continued To Mar-08-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
